pattern: Only the set of totals reachable by picking one element per row matters, and that set can be built incrementally row by row, keeping every distinct running sum instead of every individual choice.
hints: You don't need to track which element was picked from each row, only what running total is possible after each row is folded in.
Start with the single possible sum 0, and for every row, combine each currently-possible sum with each distinct value in that row to get the new set of possible sums.
After folding in every row, the answer is the smallest absolute difference between the target and any value in the final set of possible sums.

edge_inputs.description: single row, single column, exact match
single row, single column, far from target
duplicate values within a row that a naive dedup could mishandle
target falls between two achievable sums
several small rows with varied spread

stress.description: matrices near the maximum row and column counts with a random target
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    rows = rng.randint(1, 70)
    cols = rng.randint(1, 70)
    mat = [[rng.randint(1, 70) for _ in range(cols)] for _ in range(rows)]
    target = rng.randint(1, 4900)
    return [mat, target]
